item.title: Version 0.1.1
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.1 — quieter idle, snappier feel
This release is mostly performance work. Idle CPU drops to roughly nothing on Apple Silicon — the previous build had a hover-detection timer running 10 times a second whether or not anything was happening, and a search cache rebuilding itself every time any IDE window changed title.
### Performance - Dynamic Island hover monitor halves its polling rate (10 Hz → 5 Hz) and skips hit-tests entirely when the cursor isn't anywhere near the top of the screen. This is the biggest win — most ticks now exit on a single coordinate compare. - Search caches no longer rebuild while the panel is hidden. They refresh automatically the moment you open it, so badges and filter chips are always current. - Global hotkey event tap reads cached shortcut bindings instead of touching the observable settings store on every keystroke.
### Fixes (since 0.1.0) - Dynamic Island flares now meet the bezel with a clean horizontal tangent, and the pill blends into the notch and external-display top edge. - Search result rows no longer drift when the same project appears under multiple paths. - Settings detail pane no longer picks up the accent tint on its form background. - Click-to-pin removed from the Dynamic Island; hover-out collapse is reliable again.
### Known limits - No in-app purchase yet (license server still being stood up). Trial runs unattended. - macOS 26+ only.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.2
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.2 — fixes a launch crash on second launch
A bug-fix release for a crash that some users hit on Apple Silicon Macs after using the app once: the next launch could trap inside `applicationDidFinishLaunching` and the app would never reach its first window.
### Fixes - Don't crash on launch when two configured scan folders overlap (a parent and one of its subfolders, or roots that resolve to the same target). The project scanner now drops duplicate paths before returning, which kept the Dynamic Island, search, and App Intents from tripping a duplicate-key trap when building their path-keyed lookups.
No behavior or UI changes — same 0.1.1, just no crash.

item.title: Version 0.1.3
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.3 — notch matches the Quick Switcher
The Dynamic Island now shows the same set of open IDE windows that the Quick Switcher does, with one row per actual project.
### Improvements - **DataGrip and other JetBrains-virtual projects** now appear in the notch. Previously only projects living in your configured scan folders showed up, so DataGrip sessions (and any project opened from outside your scan roots) silently went missing even when the Switcher could see them. - **Same-named projects at different paths render as separate rows.** Two `Swee` projects in different folders no longer collapse into a single ambiguous row whose icon belonged to whichever scan-folder iteration order won. - **Each open window gets its own icon in the row's strip.** Two Cursor windows of the same project show as two Cursor icons — each focusable independently — instead of being deduplicated into one.
### Fixes - Hovering one row no longer highlights another. Variable row heights from a missing subtitle line, plus a couple of paths that produced colliding row IDs, were causing multiple rows to light up at once.

item.title: Version 0.1.4
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.4 — same-name projects, everywhere
When two projects share a name (e.g. an `odak.fyi` checkout in `~/code/` and another in `~/code/configs/`), Odak now shows the disambiguating parent folder as a small chip next to the project name on every surface, not just the search results.
### Improvements - **Parent-folder chip in the Quick Switcher and Dynamic Island.** Two windows or rows for same-named projects in different folders now show a `configs` / `code` pill next to the title, the same one you've seen in search. The notch uses a higher-contrast variant so the pill stays readable on the dark notch surface in light mode.
### Fixes - **Xcode-opened projects now match their scanned project correctly.** Opening Odak (or any Xcode project whose inner source directory shares the project name) used to produce two rows in the notch — one for the scanned project, one phantom row labelled like `Odak Odak` — and the search row would lose its Xcode icon. Windows resolved into a subdirectory of a scanned project now fold into that project everywhere.

item.title: Version 0.1.5
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.5 — paid licensing, smoother onboarding, better notch & switcher
This release brings the licensing flow online (Polar checkout, in-app license activation), tightens onboarding around the Accessibility prompt, and fixes a long list of papercuts across the notch, switcher, and search.
### New - **Polar checkout.** Buy a license directly from the in-app paywall — debug builds hit the staging URL, releases hit `/buy`, so you can't accidentally land on the wrong page. The Settings → License tab has been redesigned with a hero card and per-device row. - **Dedicated Settings tab for the notch.** The Dynamic Island now has its own tab in Settings with a modifier-key picker and a live enable toggle, so you can change behavior without quitting the app. - **Detect non-git projects.** The scanner now picks up projects without a `.git` folder using a tiered marker scheme (`package.json`, `Package.swift`, `Cargo.toml`, `pyproject.toml`, etc.), so JetBrains scratch projects and language workspaces show up alongside your repos.
### Improvements - **Smoother onboarding for Accessibility.** The AX-grant step now survives relaunches and revoke cycles cleanly — no more half-stuck states after granting and re-denying. - **Search starts faster.** Removed a 40ms keystroke debounce that was making the first character feel laggy. Searching now reacts as you type. - **Search field shows a Settings hint.** When the search field is empty, the hint bar surfaces a clickable `⌘, Settings` shortcut so first-time users have an obvious way in. - **Reliable Option+Space on launch.** Fixes a race where the global hotkey occasionally wouldn't register on first launch, and the search field would flash an I-beam before showing. - **Cleaner Settings copy.** Friendlier shortcut labels, better button text in Actions and Projects tabs, no scrollbar flash on tab switch. - **Palette-matched search icon.** Replaced the system magnifying glass with the brand mark — fewer visual conflicts with macOS's Spotlight. - **Switcher pins same-name windows to the right folder.** Two `index.tsx` open in two different projects now route to the correct project row based on which file is open.
### Privacy - **Max-privacy PostHog config.** Anonymous-only mode is on by default — no email identification, all event properties sanitized at the SDK boundary. Coded reasons replace free-form strings in license error events.
### Fixes - **Paywall stays on-screen.** The buy/license windows no longer drift partially off the display when opened from a corner. - **Notch hover surfaces the AX prompt** when permissions are missing, instead of silently doing nothing. - **Privacy → Enable button alignment** in onboarding now matches the rest of the step.

item.title: Version 0.1.5
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.5 (build 9) — small Quick Switcher fix
A respin of 0.1.5 with one user-facing fix.
### Fixes - **Copy Path / Copy Name now dismisses Odak.** Both copy actions used to close the actions panel but leave the search window hovering on screen, so you'd Cmd+V into a still-open Odak. They now hide the panel after the copy fires — same behavior as Open in IDE, Reveal in Finder, and the action shortcuts.

item.title: Version 0.1.6
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.6 — calmer launch, snappier switcher, prettier notch on external displays
A handful of polish fixes plus a small visual unification.
### Improvements - **Dynamic Island silhouette matches the MacBook notch on external displays.** On non-notched displays, the expanded pill now uses the same concave-flared shape with horizontal tangents as the built-in display — the shape animates open from a degenerate rest pill, so the morph reads as a single object rather than two different ones depending on which screen you're on. - **Quick Switcher Cmd+W feels instant on slow apps.** Closing a window now runs the AX press off the main actor with a 1-second messaging cap, so a hung target app no longer queues up your next Cmd+W or arrow keystroke behind a 6-second timeout.
### Fixes - **No more I-beam flash or position jump when Odak appears.** The search window used to briefly flicker at the SwiftUI default position with a text-cursor before teleporting to its anchor — caused by the off-screen parking workaround. Now it pre-positions correctly at the spotlight anchor and stays hit-test-disabled across the transparent phase, so the cursor never picks up the field's I-beam. - **License no longer drops when switching between Debug and Release builds.** Stage and prod tokens used to share one keychain slot, so opening a Debug build after a `make install` (or vice-versa) silently wiped the active license. Each environment now gets its own slot, and existing prod activations migrate automatically — you should never see "re-enter your license" again unless you actually need to.
### Other - **Pricing now reads $19** to match odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.7
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.7 — your Mac's name stays on your Mac
A small privacy tightening to how Odak's license server handles your devices.
### Privacy - **Odak no longer stores your Mac's name on the license server.** The licensing system used to send your Mac's name (the one in System Settings → Sharing — often "Your Name's MacBook") to keep track of devices on your license. It now only sends a hardware identifier and your macOS version. Your Mac's name stays on this Mac. - **"At your Mac limit" picker now labels other Macs by macOS version and last-seen.** Previously the picker showed each Mac's name; now you'll see entries like "Another Mac · macOS 26.3.0 · last seen 3 days ago", with the current Mac still clearly marked "This Mac".
### Under the hood - License server schema updated to drop the device-name columns. Existing names were removed during the migration. No action needed on your part — the change is invisible unless you hit the device-limit picker.

item.title: Version 0.1.8
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.8 — the Quick Switcher now sees every Space
Until now the switcher only listed IDE windows on the Space you were currently looking at. Editors that live on another desktop or full-screen Space — especially Electron-based ones like Cursor and VS Code — quietly went missing. This release makes the switcher show your open IDE windows across **all** Spaces, and focusing one jumps you straight to it.
### New - **IDE windows from every Space appear in the Quick Switcher.** A Cursor or VS Code window parked on another desktop now shows up alongside your current-Space windows, with its real title, and selecting it raises the window and switches you to its Space. No Screen Recording permission required.
### Under the hood - Off-Space windows are resolved once and cached for their lifetime, so the steady-state cost is a couple of accessibility reads per window rather than a scan on every refresh. Each read is bounded by a short timeout so a hung editor can't stall the switcher, and the resolver logs a warning if a future macOS update ever changes the underlying behavior — so it fails loudly instead of silently reverting to current-Space-only.
### Known limits - macOS 26+ only.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.9
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.9 — onboarding that sets you up right
Odak can only find your projects once it knows where they live, so first-run onboarding now makes that one step count — and drops you straight into search the moment you're done.
### New - **Onboarding requires at least one project folder.** The final step now waits until you've picked a folder before letting you finish, with an inline hint so it's clear why. Your common dev folders (`code`, `Developer`, `Projects`, …) are still auto-detected and pre-selected, so most setups sail right through. - **Search opens automatically after onboarding.** Finishing setup now lands you in the search panel every time, instead of occasionally leaving you on an empty desktop on a fresh install.
### Polish - The Odak mark now sits in the top-right of the onboarding window, balancing the window chrome and matching your chosen accent.
### Known limits - macOS 26+ only.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.10
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.10 — smoother switching, a sharper notch
A polish pass on the two surfaces you touch most: the window switcher's close motion and the notch's selection look.
### Polish - **Closing a window in the switcher now moves as one motion.** Whether you use ⌥W or the per-row × button, the row collapse and the panel resize now happen in the same beat — no more "the content vanished, then the window slid up to catch it." The closing row also fades out cleanly instead of briefly ghosting over the row taking its place. - **The notch highlights with your accent color.** Hovering a project in the notch now paints the row in your chosen accent palette — the same selection color the quick switcher uses — instead of a flat gray. - **The notch's bottom row sits flush in the corner.** The selection highlight now nests concentrically into the notch card's rounded bottom, matching how the switcher's bottom row hugs its panel, instead of leaving a curved shoulder around a squarer row.
### Known limits - macOS 26+ only.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.11
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.11 — hold Right ⌘ to find any window, now on macOS 15
This release adds a second, faster way to reach a window — hold Right ⌘ and just type — and broadens Odak to run on macOS 15 Sequoia, not only Tahoe.
### New - **Hold Right ⌘, type, release — land in the window.** A new rcmd-style trigger sits alongside ⌥Tab: hold the right Command key, type a few letters to fuzzy-match your open IDE windows, and release to switch. Numbered rows still work, so ⌘1–9 jumps straight to a result. The classic ⌥Tab switcher is unchanged. - **Runs on macOS 15 Sequoia.** Odak previously needed macOS 26 for its Liquid Glass panel. It now falls back to a native material on Sequoia, so the full app works on macOS 15 and later — and still lights up with Liquid Glass on macOS 26. - **Open a project straight in your editor from search.** Hover a search result and click the IDE icon to open it in your editor, without switching to an existing window first.
### Polish - The type-to-search field renders the query as a single text run, so letters no longer jitter or shift as you type. The typed text is also crisper and higher-contrast in light mode. - Row hover is now driven from a single source of truth, fixing cases where the switcher and the result list could disagree about which row was highlighted, and the selection indicator is correctly centered.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.12
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.12 — windows on other Spaces show up reliably
The switcher now finds IDE windows that live on other desktops (Spaces) — including a second window of the same project — that could previously go missing.
### Fixed - **Windows on other Spaces now appear in the switcher.** When a window lived on a different desktop than the one you were on, the switcher could miss it — most often a Cursor or VS Code window whose internal accessibility handle fell just past Odak's off-Space lookup limit. Off-Space windows are now resolved reliably, and the result is cached so refreshes stay fast. - **Two windows of the same project no longer collapse into one row.** When the system couldn't hand Odak a stable window identifier (minimized windows, and some Electron windows), two windows of the same project in the same editor could fold into a single entry. They're now kept distinct.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.13
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.13 — no more freezes during window activity, rescan works instantly
A responsiveness release: Odak no longer stalls when editor windows open or close, and a project rescan shows up in search right away.
### Fixed - **Odak no longer freezes while your IDE is busy.** Refreshing the window list talked to each editor synchronously on the main thread — if Cursor, VS Code, or a JetBrains IDE was mid-build or indexing, opening or closing one of its windows could lock Odak up for seconds. The whole window scan now runs in the background with a tight per-app timeout, and the switcher opens instantly with the latest list folding in the moment the scan lands. - **Rescan now updates search immediately.** Pressing Rescan in Settings (or the automatic background rescan picking up a freshly cloned repo) rebuilt the project index but search kept serving the old list until you relaunched Odak. New projects now appear in search the moment the scan finishes — no relaunch needed.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.14
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.14 — move the search panel anywhere, smarter search, faster launch
The search panel is no longer pinned in place: drag it wherever you like, with magnetic guides to snap it back. Plus noticeably smarter fuzzy search, a ~2x faster launch, and fixes for vanishing windows and license lockouts.
### New - **Drag the search panel anywhere.** Grab the panel and place it where you want — dashed guides appear at the default spot and the panel snaps magnetically when you get close. Drop it back on the guides (or use Settings → Search → Reset Position) to return to the default, and a new Top/Center anchor picker chooses where that default lives.
### Improved - **Smarter search ranking.** The fuzzy matcher now always finds the best-scoring alignment and understands camelCase humps, acronyms, and digit groups — "wsv" cleanly matches WindowSwitcherView. Shorter and word-boundary matches rank first, and projects and windows rank by the same rules. - **~2x faster launch.** The panel appears as soon as its window is ready instead of waiting on a fixed delay, and Odak no longer blocks launch on an IDE that's busy compiling or indexing. - **Unified look across panels.** Search, the window switcher, and the notch now share the same keycap-style shortcut hints, dividers, and empty states. Selected rows are readable on every accent palette — pale palettes like Bone and Periwinkle no longer show white-on-white text.
### Fixed - **Windows on other Spaces no longer vanish after login.** IDE windows restored to other Spaces while their apps were still starting up could disappear from the switcher until Odak restarted. Odak now retries until apps are fully awake and rescans during the first 90 seconds after startup. - **Valid licenses no longer fall back to the paywall.** A mismatch between the revalidation cadence and the offline-grace window could lock activated licenses out after 3 days. Revalidation now runs daily with a 7-day offline grace, and recovers automatically with a single online check — no re-entering your code.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.15
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.15 — the Actions Marketplace
Actions are Odak's superpower, and now you don't have to write them yourself: curated packs live at [odak.fyi/actions](https://odak.fyi/actions) and import with one click.
### New - **Actions Marketplace.** Six curated packs — Git, GitHub, GitLab, Docker, Node/npm, and Xcode/Swift — at [odak.fyi/actions](https://odak.fyi/actions). Every pack shows each action and shortcut up front; **Add to Odak** imports it after the usual confirmation dialog, and the actions append to your `config.yaml` ready to edit. - **Browse Marketplace from Settings.** Settings → Actions now links straight to the marketplace, next to Edit config.yaml and Reload. - **YAML imports.** `odak://import?url=…` now accepts YAML as well as JSON — a single action, a list, or an `actions:` list. Share a snippet of your config.yaml via a Gist and it imports verbatim.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.16
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.16 — groups, brand icons, and a real import review
The actions panel grows up: imports get a proper review panel, packs land in their own named sections, and rows finally wear the right logos.
### New - **Import review panel.** `odak://import` now opens a native review panel inside Odak — every action listed with its icon and shortcut, script actions flagged — instead of a bare confirmation dialog. Nothing is written until you approve. - **Action groups.** Actions from a pack (or a `group:` value in your config.yaml) render under their own section header in the panel, between Open and Copy, in config order. - **Brand icons.** Actions can declare an `icon:` — Git, GitHub, GitLab, Docker, and Node logos ship built in (the same set the [marketplace](https://odak.fyi/actions) shows), with any SF Symbol name as a fallback. Rows resolve the real app icon first, as before. - **Action editor.** Settings → Actions gains an add/edit sheet, so one-off tweaks no longer require opening config.yaml.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.
